# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What this repo is

GitHub special profile repository for user `saitenntaisei` — the `README.md` renders on the user's profile page. There is no application code, no build system, and no test suite. The repo is plumbing for a set of GitHub Actions that regenerate the SVG artifacts referenced by `README.md`.

## Authored vs generated files

Only these are hand-edited:

- `README.md` — the profile page markup.
- `metrics-presets/preset-1.yml`, `metrics-presets/preset-2.yml` — configuration consumed by `.github/workflows/metrics.yml`. Editing a preset and pushing to `main` triggers regeneration.
- `.github/workflows/*.yml` — the two generator workflows.

Do **not** hand-edit the following — they are overwritten on every workflow run, and edits will be clobbered within 24 hours:

- `metrics-1.svg`, `metrics-2.svg` — regenerated by `metrics.yml` (schedule: daily 00:00 UTC, on push to `main`, or manual). `metrics-1` uses `lowlighter/metrics@latest` with `preset-1`. `metrics-2` uses the `stalomeow/metrics@projects-v2` fork with `preset-2`.
- `profile-summary-card-output/**` — all 60+ theme subdirectories are regenerated by `github-profile-summary-cards.yml`.
- `dist/` — build-output staging for the `Platane/snk` snake graph; only `.gitkeep` is tracked on `main`. The generated `github-snake*.svg` files are pushed to the separate `output` branch via `crazy-max/ghaction-github-pages`.

## The `output` branch

`README.md` pulls the snake animation via raw.githubusercontent.com URLs pointing at the `output` branch (not `main`). If you change the snake filenames in `metrics.yml`, update the corresponding `srcset`/`src` URLs in `README.md` at the same time. Likewise, switching the theme directory (e.g., `dracula` → another) in `profile-summary-card-output/` requires editing the image paths in `README.md`.

## Secrets and permissions

Both workflows read `secrets.METRICS_TOKEN` — a Personal Access Token, **not** `GITHUB_TOKEN`. It is passed as `token:` for the metrics actions and as `GITHUB_TOKEN:` env for the snake/pages step. The PAT must have `gist`, `repo`, `read:org`, and `read:user` scopes; missing scopes silently degrade individual plugins rather than failing the workflow.

## Loop prevention

Bot commits use the subject `Update metrics-*.svg - [Skip GitHub Action]` / `Generate profile summary cards`. The workflows have no `if:` guard on the commit message; loops are prevented by the PAT push-trigger semantics (self-push via a PAT can re-trigger, but the workflow concurrency group `metrics-${{ github.ref }}` with `cancel-in-progress: true` bounds it). If you add a new workflow that also commits to `main`, add a similar concurrency group to avoid cascades.

## Testing changes

There is no local execution path for these actions — they build a Docker image on the runner. To validate a preset change, push to `main` and watch the `Metrics` workflow run in GitHub Actions. To read the actual plugin errors (the generated SVG only shows a generic "Unexpected error" tile), pull the job log:

```
gh run list --workflow=metrics.yml --limit 3
gh api repos/saitenntaisei/saitenntaisei/actions/jobs/<job_id>/logs | grep -iE "error|unhandled" -C 3
```
